title: Crumbly Canadian Lamb Kidney Hash
description: A hearty, herbal Canadian dish featuring lamb kidney, perfect for a comforting meal, and diabetic-friendly due to its low carb content.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the cauliflower florets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the cauliflower mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
While the cauliflower is roasting, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the katsuramuki-cut lamb kidney to the skillet, cooking until it is browned on all sides and cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the dried thyme and rosemary, cooking for 1 minute to allow the herbs to infuse into the dish.
To serve, top the roasted cauliflower with the lamb kidney mixture and serve hot.
